fix: prevent zero-cost purchase — coupon CAS, min order amount, amount tolerance

- Coupon use_coupon() now has CAS guard: used_count < max_uses in the
  UPDATE WHERE clause, preventing concurrent over-use
- Coupon discount capped at 95% of subtotal (was 100%)
- Percentage coupon creation capped at 95% (was 100%)
- Minimum order amount enforced at ¥0.10 (was ¥0.01)
- Quantity must be >= 1 (explicit check)
- Coupon usage failure now propagated as error (was silently ignored)
- Payment callback amount tolerance changed from fixed ±0.01 to
  relative max(2%, ¥0.01) — prevents tiny-amount bypass

commit.message:
fontconfig gnome-49: add -Dcache-dir=/usr/lib/fontconfig/cache

Fixes Flatpak apps not discovering host fonts (GH#21).
Without this flag, fc-cache writes to /var/cache/fontconfig which
is lost during container builds. The persistent path
/usr/lib/fontconfig/cache is exposed to Flatpak sandboxes via
/run/host/fonts-cache/.

Fix duplicate models and Ollama 404 on chat

- ListModelsEndpoint: add DistinctBy(m => m.Name) to both the primary
  and fallback model lists to prevent duplicates when AllowedModels
  contains the same entry twice (e.g. via env var concatenation).

  404 for untagged names (e.g. 'llama3.1'); the full tag is required

commit.message:
Security hardening for Phase 4: Hash API keys, improve path validation, enhance command injection detection

- SECURITY-001: Implement Argon2/PBKDF2 hashing for API keys (was: plaintext)
  - Add _hash_key() and _verify_hashed_key() with constant-time comparison
  - Automatic migration of existing plaintext keys on startup
  - File permissions set to 0o600 (owner read/write only)
  - New dependency: argon2-cffi>=23.0.0

- SECURITY-002: Fix path traversal validation in terminal tool
  - Replace naive '..' splitting with os.path.realpath() resolution
  - Validate that resolved paths exist and are directories
  - Consistent with filesystem tools' path handling

- SECURITY-003: Enhance command injection prevention
  - Expand DANGEROUS_PATTERNS blocklist (11 patterns now)
  - Add netcat/bash pipe detection
  - Add /dev/zero and /dev/tcp detection
  - New STRICT_COMMAND_MODE env var for allowlist-only execution

- SECURITY-004: Add comprehensive security test suite
  - 4 path traversal prevention tests
  - 9 command injection prevention tests
  - 3 API key hashing tests
  - Total: 16 new security-focused tests

All changes are backward-compatible. Existing installations automatically
migrate plaintext keys to hashes on startup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: security round 4 — transaction safety, token-based auth, post-action reliability

A. Withdrawal transaction + CAS: wrap balance deduction + withdrawal insert
   in a single DB transaction with conditional UPDATE to prevent overdraft.

B. Order query_token: add per-order high-entropy token for public queries.
   Payment return redirect includes token for seamless auto-query.
   Email-based order listing removed; legacy email fallback only for
   pre-token orders.

C. AFF query by code: public AFF endpoints now use AFF code (16-char)
   instead of email. Prevents email enumeration.

D. Post-action status tracking: orders track post_action_status
   (pending/success/failed). Failed actions auto-retry up to 3 times
   via cleanup task. Orders don't reach false-positive delivered state.

E. Frontend super_admin guard: route-level meta + beforeEach check.
   403 responses handled distinctly from 401 (permission denied vs
   unauthorized).

F. Tests: 6 id_gen tests covering query_token generation, AFF code
   length, and uniqueness.

Files changed: 22 files across backend services, entities, migrations,
API routes, frontend views, router, i18n, and API client.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Introduces docs/advanced_memory_strategy.md — a structured usage pattern
for teams using MemPalace as a governed knowledge layer across multiple
projects and AI agents.

Problem addressed:
- Unstructured stores accumulate duplicate, stale, and mis-scoped entries
- Agents lose context across sessions without a retrieval discipline

This is an example advanced usage pattern, not a required or official
workflow. Existing docs are untouched.

commit.message:
Improve Ashes backend: DWARF language identification, debug info determinism, and IR optimizations (#64)

* Replace byte-by-byte store loops with global constant + memcpy

Add LLVM API bindings for ConstArray2, SetGlobalConstant, and
SetUnnamedAddr. Replace three byte-by-byte store loops with
global constant arrays + LLVMBuildMemCpy:

- EmitHeapStringFromBytes: heap string literals
- EmitStackStringObject: stack string objects for output
- EmitStackByteArray: stack byte arrays for I/O

This reduces IR instruction count proportional to string length
and allows LLVM to optimize the copy to vectorized instructions.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/MattiasHognas/Ashes/sessions/678ee9e1-e092-45e8-80fb-51baef66e9b5

Co-authored-by: MattiasHognas <1923344+MattiasHognas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Place string literals in .rodata as global constants

EmitHeapStringLiteral now creates a global constant struct
{ i64 length, [N x i8] data } in the read-only data section
instead of heap-allocating and copying bytes at runtime.

Since Ashes strings are immutable, string literals never need
heap allocation. This eliminates heap pressure for all string
literals and makes their addresses available at link time.

Added LLVM API bindings for ConstStructInContext and
StructTypeInContext to support creating typed struct constants.

commit.message:
Improve highlighting responsivity and fix pause/resume position

Add a spoken cursor powered by the Speech SDK recognizing (interim) event
for near-real-time word tracking during reading sessions.

Highlighting improvements:
- Add recognizer.recognizing handler to speechService for interim results
- Track spokenCursor in useAssessment that advances word-by-word as the
  child speaks, independent of final scored results
- Add isBeingRead visual state to WordCard (subtle indigo tint for the
  zone between last scored word and spoken cursor)
- Speed up next-word pulse animation from 1.8s to 1.0s

Pause/resume fix:
- resumeListening now uses Math.max(scoredPosition, spokenCursor) so the
  child never has to re-read words they already spoke before pausing
- Filter false omissions during stop: when the recognizer is stopping,

Bump @eslint/js from 9.39.4 to 10.0.1 in /src/vscode-bicep and fix new lint errors (#19366)

Upgrades `@eslint/js` to 10.0.1 (major bump) in `src/vscode-bicep`. The
new version introduces two additional rules in `recommended` —
`no-useless-assignment` and `preserve-caught-error` — which flag
existing code.

## Changes

- **`deploy.ts`**: Replace useless initial assignments (`= undefined`,
`= "None"`) with explicit type annotations
- **`generateParams.ts`, `testScope.ts`, `AzurePickers.ts`**: Add `{
cause: err }` to re-thrown errors to satisfy `preserve-caught-error`
- **`surveys.ts`**: Add `eslint-disable-next-line no-useless-assignment`
— the `"unknown"` initial value is required by TypeScript's control flow
analysis for the `finally` block
- **`tsconfig.json`**: Add `ES2022.Error` to `lib` to enable TypeScript
support for the `Error` constructor's `cause` option

```ts
// Before
throw new Error(`Generating parameters failed: ${parseError(err).message}`);

// After
throw new Error(`Generating parameters failed: ${parseError(err).message}`, { cause: err });
```

* fix: normalize warning_pct to 2 decimal places for lossless UI round-trip

The frontend form uses integers 0-100 but the backend stores 0-1 decimal.
Values with excess precision (e.g., 0.3333) would lose data on edit via
Math.round(). Adding a Pydantic field_validator with ROUND_HALF_UP on both
LimitCreate and LimitUpdate ensures the stored value always maps cleanly
to a whole percentage.
